questions.text: For n = 63 nodes, what is the best-case (perfectly balanced) height?
questions.explanation: 63 sits just below 64 = 2^6, so a perfectly balanced tree of 63 nodes is a complete binary tree with 6 levels, root at depth 0 through the deepest leaves at depth 5. Height 5.

questions.text: For the same n = 63 nodes, what is the worst-case (degenerate) height?
questions.explanation: A degenerate tree is a single chain of all 63 nodes, so its height is n minus 1: 62.
